html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 钦定四库全书 - Imperially Approved Complete Library, a major collection of Chinese books compiled during the Qing dynasty.
  • 子部十二 - The eleventh of twelve major categories in the traditional Chinese classification system for books, which includes various types of literature.
  • 小説家 - Refers to the category of 'novelists' or 'storytellers' within the literary classification.
  • 杂事之属 - Indicates a subcategory of miscellaneous stories or anecdotes.
  • 提要 - Means 'introduction' or 'summary,' often used to provide an overview of the content.
  • 【臣】等谨案 - A formal opening indicating that the text is written by officials presenting a report to the emperor.
  • 官至检校刑部尚书 - Means 'officially reached the position of Censor-in-Inspector of the Ministry of Justice.'
  • 宣武军节度使 - Refers to a military governorship in the Tang dynasty.
  • 事迹附见旧唐书郑余庆传 - Means 'the biography is attached to the Old Book of Tang's entry on Zheng Yuqing.'
  • 载所闻杂事 - Means 'records various miscellaneous events.'
  • 案史称 - Means 'according to historical records.'
  • 撰次明皇杂录三篇 - Means 'compiled three volumes of miscellaneous records about Emperor Ming.'
  • 行于世 - Means 'circulated in the world.'
  • 晁公武读书志 - Refers to a bibliographic work by Zhao Gongwu.
  • 别录一卷 - Means 'a separate volume.'
  • 题补阙所载十二事 - Means 'the twelve events recorded by the Censor-in-Inspector.'
  • 叶梦得避暑录话 - Refers to a work by Ye Mengde.
  • 张曲江与李林甫争牛仙客实封 - Refers to a historical event involving Zhang Qujiang and Li Linfu.
  • 惶恐作赋以献 - Means 'feeling anxious, he wrote a poem to present.'
  • 意若言明皇以忤旨将废黜 - Means 'as if saying that Emperor Ming was about to be dismissed for disobeying orders.'
  • 新书取载之本传 - Means 'the New Book of Tang included it in the biography.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 钦定四库全书 - The Imperially Approved Complete Library, a vast collection of Chinese texts compiled during the Qing dynasty.
  • 子部十二 - The eleventh category in the traditional Chinese classification system for books.
  • 小説家 - The category of novelists or storytellers.
  • 杂事之属 - Miscellaneous stories or anecdotes.
  • 提要 - Introduction or summary.
  • 【臣】等谨案 - Formal opening indicating an official report.
  • 官至检校刑部尚书 - High-ranking official position.
  • 宣武军节度使 - Military governorship.
  • 事迹附见旧唐书郑余庆传 - Biography in the Old Book of Tang.
  • 载所闻杂事 - Records of various events.
  • 案史称 - According to historical records.
  • 撰次明皇杂录三篇 - Compiled three volumes.
  • 行于世 - Circulated widely.
  • 晁公武读书志 - Bibliographic work by Zhao Gongwu.
  • 别录一卷 - Separate volume.
  • 题补阙所载十二事 - Events recorded by the Censor-in-Inspector.
  • 叶梦得避暑录话 - Work by Ye Mengde.
  • 张曲江与李林甫争牛仙客实封 - Historical event involving Zhang Qujiang and Li Linfu.
  • 惶恐作赋以献 - Poem written in response.
  • 意若言明皇以忤旨将废黜 - Implication of dismissal.
  • 新书取载之本传 - Inclusion in the New Book of Tang.

Proper Nouns

  • 钦定四库全书 - The Imperially Approved Complete Library.
  • 子部十二 - The eleventh category in the traditional Chinese classification system.
  • 小説家 - The category of novelists or storytellers.
  • 杂事之属 - Miscellaneous stories or anecdotes.
  • 提要 - Introduction or summary.
  • 【臣】等谨案 - Formal opening indicating an official report.
  • 官至检校刑部尚书 - High-ranking official position.
  • 宣武军节度使 - Military governorship.
  • 事迹附见旧唐书郑余庆传 - Biography in the Old Book of Tang.
  • 载所闻杂事 - Records of various events.
  • 案史称 - According to historical records.
  • 撰次明皇杂录三篇 - Compiled three volumes.
  • 行于世 - Circulated widely.
  • 晁公武读书志 - Bibliographic work by Zhao Gongwu.
  • 别录一卷 - Separate volume.
  • 题补阙所载十二事 - Events recorded by the Censor-in-Inspector.
  • 叶梦得避暑录话 - Work by Ye Mengde.
  • 张曲江与李林甫争牛仙客实封 - Historical event involving Zhang Qujiang and Li Linfu.
  • 惶恐作赋以献 - Poem written in response.
  • 意若言明皇以忤旨将废黜 - Implication of dismissal.
  • 新书取载之本传 - Inclusion in the New Book of Tang.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 據曲江集賦序曰 - Formal way of introducing a source: 'According to the preface of the *Qujiang Collection*.'
  • 奉勅 - Formal expression indicating an official order from the emperor: 'received imperial decree.'
  • 大将军 - High military rank: 'Grand General.'
  • 髙力士 - Name of a prominent eunuch during the Tang Dynasty: 'Gao Lishi.'
  • 宰相 - Highest-ranking official in the imperial government: 'Chancellor.'
  • 白羽扇 - Symbol of authority and status: 'white feather fan.'
  • 通言宰相 - Referring to the group of Chancellors collectively: 'generally speaking, Chancellors.'
  • 不独为曲江而设也 - Indicating that something is not limited to a specific person or place: 'not exclusively for Qujiang.'
  • 小説记事 - Narratives found in literary works: 'stories in novels.'
  • 耳目亲接 - Emphasizing firsthand knowledge: 'direct personal experience.'
  • 轻书耶 - Suggesting a lack of careful documentation: 'lightly recorded.'
  • 云云 - Referring back to previously stated information: 'as mentioned above.'
  • 不尽实录 - Indicating that the account is not completely accurate: 'not entirely factual.'
  • 通言 - Introducing a general statement: 'generally speaking.'
  • 妄信之 - Indicating a mistaken belief: 'erroneously believed.'
  • 避暑录话 - Title of a historical text: 'Records of Summer Retreat.'
  • 卢懐慎 - Historical figure: 'Lu Huishen.'
  • 好俭 - Description of a person's lifestyle: 'practicing frugality.'
  • 无珠玉锦绣之饰 - Describing a simple and modest lifestyle: 'without ornaments of precious gems, jade, silk, or embroidery.'
  • 史言 - Referring to what is documented in official histories: 'historical records state.'
  • 妻子至寒饿 - Describing a state of poverty: 'his wife and children were cold and hungry.'
  • 宋璟等过之门不施箔 - Indicating a lack of privacy: 'when Cui Yan and others passed by his door, they did not use screens.'
  • 风雨至引席自障 - Describing a simple and humble living condition: 'when it rained or snowed, they would pull out mats to shield themselves.'
  • 盖出 - Indicating the origin of a piece of information: 'probably derived from.'
  • 明皇杂录 - Title of a historical text: 'Miscellaneous Records of Emperor Ming.'
  • 史臣 - Referring to those who wrote official histories: 'historians.'
  • 已有所佚脱 - Suggesting that the text is incomplete: 'some parts have been lost.'
  • 完帙 - Referring to a fully intact book: 'complete volume.'
  • 乾隆四十六年十二月 - Specific date in the Qianlong era: 'December of the 46th year of Qianlong's reign.'
  • 恭校上 - Formal way of presenting a text for review: 'respectfully proofread and submitted.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 曲江集 - Literary anthology: 'Qujiang Collection.'
  • 开元二十四年 - Specific year during the Tang Dynasty: 'the 24th year of Kaiyuan.'
  • 盛夏 - Time of year: 'midsummer.'
  • 白羽扇 - Symbol of authority and status: 'white feather fan.'
  • 宋璟 - Chancellor during the Tang Dynasty: 'Song Jing.'
  • 明皇杂录 - Historical text: 'Miscellaneous Records of Emperor Ming.'
  • 避暑录话 - Historical text: 'Records of Summer Retreat.'
  • 乾隆四十六年 - Specific year during the Qing Dynasty: 'the 46th year of Qianlong's reign.'

Proper Nouns

  • 曲江集 - Literary anthology: 'Qujiang Collection.'
  • 宋璟 - Chancellor during the Tang Dynasty: 'Song Jing.'
  • 明皇杂录 - Historical text: 'Miscellaneous Records of Emperor Ming.'
  • 避暑录话 - Historical text: 'Records of Summer Retreat.'
  • 乾隆四十六年 - Specific year during the Qing Dynasty: 'the 46th year of Qianlong's reign.'

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 總纂官 - Title meaning 'general editor' or 'chief compiler.'
  • 總校官 - Title meaning 'chief proofreader' or 'chief examiner.'
  • 欽定四庫全書 - Title of a major imperial compilation project, literally 'Imperially Ordered Four Treasuries of Literature.'
  • 明皇雜録卷上 - Title of the text, meaning 'Miscellaneous Records of the Ming Emperor, Volume One.'
  • 鄭處诲撰 - Author's name, meaning 'compiled by Zheng Chuhui.'
  • 開元中 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Xuanzong during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 房琯之宰盧氏也 - Means 'when Fang Guan was the magistrate of Lü County.'
  • 虛心禮敬 - Means 'to treat with utmost respect and courtesy.'
  • 攅椟於宮中 - Means 'to place the coffin in the temple.'
  • 棺得梓木為之 - Means 'the coffin was made of paulownia wood.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 房琯 - A prominent Tang Dynasty official and poet.
  • 邢真和璞 - A renowned astrologer and diviner of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 夏谷村 - A village name, likely a fictional or historical location.
  • 廢佛堂 - An abandoned Buddhist temple.
  • 松竹森映 - Describes a scene with dense pine and bamboo trees.
  • 和璞 - Another name for Xing Zhen Hépú.
  • 娄师得与永公书 - Letters written by Lou Shide and Yong Gong, likely historical figures.
  • 永公 - A Buddhist monk, possibly a historical figure.
  • 梓木 - Paulownia wood, known for its lightweight and durability.
  • 紫极宫 - A Taoist temple, possibly a real or fictional location.
  • 阆州 - A prefecture in Sichuan Province during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 暴卒 - Sudden death.
  • 攅椟 - To place the coffin.

Proper Nouns

  • 房琯 - Fang Guan, a Tang Dynasty official and poet.
  • 邢真和璞 - Xing Zhen Hépú, a Tang Dynasty astrologer and diviner.
  • 夏谷村 - A village name, likely a fictional or historical location.
  • 廢佛堂 - An abandoned Buddhist temple.
  • 松竹森映 - A descriptive phrase for a scene with dense pine and bamboo trees.
  • 和璞 - Another name for Xing Zhen Hépú.
  • 娄师得与永公书 - Letters written by Lou Shide and Yong Gong, likely historical figures.
  • 永公 - A Buddhist monk, possibly a historical figure.
  • 梓木 - Paulownia wood, known for its lightweight and durability.
  • 紫极宫 - A Taoist temple, possibly a real or fictional location.
  • 阆州 - A prefecture in Sichuan Province during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 暴卒 - Sudden death.
  • 攅椟 - To place the coffin.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 開元末 - Refers to the end of the Kaiyuan era, a period during the Tang Dynasty known for its prosperity and cultural achievements.
  • 善相人 - Means 'skilled in physiognomy,' referring to the practice of predicting a person's fate or character from their appearance.
  • 僚吏 - Refers to local officials or government servants.
  • 台輔 - A term for high-ranking officials, often used to describe those who hold positions in the imperial court.
  • 神器大寳 - Refers to the symbols of imperial power, such as the throne or the emperor's seal.
  • 合在掌握中 - Means 'should be within one's grasp' or 'should be in one's control.'
  • 後合為 - Means 'will eventually become' or 'will later serve as.'
  • 署其子為牙將 - Means 'appoint his son as a military officer.'
  • 以粟帛赈防其家 - Means 'to provide grain and silk to support his family.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 開元 - The reign title of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, marking a period of significant cultural and economic development.
  • 杭州 - Refers to Hangzhou, a city in Zhejiang Province, known for its scenic beauty and historical significance.
  • 睦州 - Refers to Muzhou, a prefecture in Zhejiang Province during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 房琯 - A prominent official during the Tang Dynasty, known for his role in the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 崔涣 - A Tang Dynasty official who served as the governor of Tonglu County after being demoted from a higher position.
  • 萬年縣尉 - Refers to a county magistrate in the area around Chang'an (modern-day Xi'an).
  • 桐廬丞 - Refers to the deputy governor of Tonglu County.
  • 靈武 - Refers to Lingwu, a city in modern-day Ningxia, where Emperor Suzong of Tang was enthroned during the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 肅宗 - The posthumous name of Emperor Suzong of Tang, who ruled during the later part of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 張嘉贞 - A Tang Dynasty official known for his administrative skills.
  • 中书侍郎 - Refers to a high-ranking official in the central government, responsible for drafting edicts and managing state affairs.
  • 韦抗 - A Tang Dynasty official who served as the Minister of Personnel.

Proper Nouns

  • 開元 - The reign title of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 杭州 - Hangzhou, a city in Zhejiang Province.
  • 睦州 - Muzhou, a prefecture in Zhejiang Province.
  • 房琯 - Fang Guan, a Tang Dynasty official.
  • 崔涣 - Cui Huan, a Tang Dynasty official.
  • 萬年縣尉 - County magistrate in the area around Chang'an.
  • 桐廬丞 - Deputy governor of Tonglu County.
  • 靈武 - Lingwu, a city in modern-day Ningxia.
  • 肅宗 - The posthumous name of Emperor Suzong of Tang.
  • 張嘉贞 - Zhang Jiazheng, a Tang Dynasty official.
  • 中书侍郎 - High-ranking official in the central government.
  • 韦抗 - Wei Kang, a Tang Dynasty official.
  • 张齐丘 - Zhang Qizhou, a Tang Dynasty official.
  • 朔方 - Shuofang, a region in northern China.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 成上甚稱其敏捷典麗 - The emperor highly praised his (Zhang Kang's) agility and elegance.
  • 因促命写诏勅抗归宿省中 - He then hurriedly ordered Zhang Kang to write the edict and return to the palace.
  • 上不解衣以待 - The emperor did not undress and waited.
  • 且将降其诏书 - He was about to issue the edict.
  • 夜漏未半 - It was still early in the night.
  • 忽有中人复促抗入见 - Suddenly, a court eunuch came to summon Zhang Kang again.
  • 上迎谓曰 - The emperor greeted him and said.
  • 非张齐丘乃太原节度张嘉贞别命草诏 - It was not Zhang Qi, but Zhang Jiachen, the military governor of Taiyuan, who was specifically tasked with drafting the edict.
  • 上谓抗曰 - The emperor said to Zhang Kang.
  • 维朕志先定可以言命矣 - Only when my mind is set can I speak and issue orders.
  • 适朕因阅近日大臣章疏首举一通乃嘉贞表也因此洒然方记得其名 - Just now, while reviewing recent memorials from the ministers, I came across one from Zhang Jiachen, and only then did I remember his name.
  • 此亦天啓非人事也 - This is also divinely inspired, not due to human effort.
  • 上嘉其得人 - The emperor praised his ability to find the right person.
  • 复叹用舍如有人主张 - He also sighed that appointments and dismissals should be made by someone with authority.
  • 苏颋聪悟过人 - Su Ting was exceptionally intelligent and perceptive.
  • 日诵数千言 - He could read thousands of words in a day.
  • 虽记览如神而父瓌训励严至常令衣青布襦伏于牀下出其颈受榎楚 - Although he had a remarkable memory, his father, Su Gui, was very strict and often made him wear a blue cotton shirt and lie on the bed, exposing his neck to receive punishment.
  • 及壮而文学该博冠于一时 - By the time he grew up, he had become a leading scholar of his time.
  • 性疎俊嗜酒 - He was naturally carefree and enjoyed drinking.
  • 瓌曰臣不知其他臣男颋甚敏捷可备指使然嗜酒幸免沾醉足以了其事 - Su Gui replied, 'I don't know about others, but my son Su Ting is very agile and can be relied upon. However, he enjoys drinking, so please ensure he doesn't get drunk, and he can handle the task.'
  • 既醒受简笔立成才藻纵横词 - After he sobered up, he took the paper and wrote it down immediately, producing a splendid and eloquent text.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 成上 - Refers to the emperor, likely Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 张齐丘 - Zhang Qi, a person mentioned in the text.
  • 太原节度张嘉贞 - Zhang Jiachen, the military governor of Taiyuan.
  • 苏颋 - Su Ting, a prominent scholar and official during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 内难 - Internal turmoil, likely referring to the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 制书 - An imperial edict.
  • 宿酲 - A hangover, indicating that Su Ting was still affected by alcohol.
  • 中人 - Court eunuchs, who were often involved in palace affairs.
  • 榎楚 - A type of punishment, often involving flogging.
  • 青布襦 - A blue cotton shirt, indicating a simple and humble attire.
  • 牀下 - Under the bed, where Su Gui made his son lie.
  • 粗备拜舞 - A clumsy bow, indicating that Su Ting was still affected by his hangover.
  • 才藻纵横词 - A text that is splendid and eloquent, indicating high literary quality.

Proper Nouns

  • 成上 - The emperor, likely Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 张齐丘 - Zhang Qi, a person mentioned in the text.
  • 太原节度张嘉贞 - Zhang Jiachen, the military governor of Taiyuan.
  • 苏颋 - Su Ting, a prominent scholar and official during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 内难 - Internal turmoil, likely referring to the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 制书 - An imperial edict.
  • 宿酲 - A hangover.
  • 中人 - Court eunuchs.
  • 榎楚 - A type of punishment.
  • 青布襦 - A blue cotton shirt.
  • 牀下 - Under the bed.
  • 粗备拜舞 - A clumsy bow.
  • 才藻纵横词 - A text that is splendid and eloquent.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 理典赡 - Refers to a person or work being well-organized and comprehensive. The exact meaning may depend on context, but it suggests a high level of organization or detail.
  • 知子莫若父 - Common idiom meaning 'no one knows a son better than his father,' often used to express the father's deep understanding of his son.
  • 大用 - Literally 'great use,' but in this context, it likely refers to a high-ranking position or significant role in governance.
  • 三絶 - Refers to three exceptional talents or achievements, often used to describe a person who excels in three different areas.
  • 善八分书 - '善' means 'skilled in,' and '八分书' refers to a style of calligraphy known as 'Eight-Stroke Script,' a variant of Han Dynasty clerical script.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 理典赡 - This term may refer to a person or work that is well-organized and comprehensive, possibly in the context of governance or literature. The exact meaning may require further historical context.
  • 韦嗣立拜中书令 - Refers to Wei Sili being appointed as the Grand Secretary (中书令), a high-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty government.
  • 薛稷书 - Refers to Xue Ji, a famous calligrapher of the Tang Dynasty, known for his contributions to the art of calligraphy.
  • 东明观道士周彦云 - Refers to Zhou Yanyun, a Daoist priest at Dongming Temple, who was known for his spiritual practices and possibly his influence on the court.
  • 黄文袍 - Refers to a ceremonial robe, likely a symbol of honor or recognition bestowed by the emperor.

Proper Nouns

  • 韦嗣立 - Wei Sili, a high-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty, known for his role as Grand Secretary (中书令).
  • 薛稷 - Xue Ji, a famous calligrapher of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 周彦云 - Zhou Yanyun, a Daoist priest at Dongming Temple, known for his spiritual practices.
  • 刘晏 - Liu Yan, a child prodigy in the Tang Dynasty, known for his exceptional intelligence and early recognition by the emperor.
  • 王大娘 - Wang Dainiang, a performer known for her acrobatic skills, particularly her ability to balance on a tall pole.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 舉明經 - A classical phrase meaning 'to take the Mingjing examination,' a type of imperial examination in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 黜落 - A classical phrase meaning 'to be disqualified or eliminated from the examination.'
  • 趨朝 - A classical phrase meaning 'to hurry to court.'
  • 轩盖如市 - A classical phrase meaning 'carriages and horses as numerous as in a market,' describing a bustling scene.
  • 我兒何慮不富貴 - A classical phrase meaning 'my son will surely be wealthy and honored,' expressing confidence in one's son's future.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 楊國忠 - Yang Guozhong, a prominent official during the Tang Dynasty, known for his power and influence.
  • 華清宮 - Huaqing Palace, a famous imperial resort in the Tang Dynasty, known for its luxurious settings.

Proper Nouns

  • 楊國忠 - Yang Guozhong, a prominent official during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 華清宮 - Huaqing Palace, a famous imperial resort in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 防昌尉 - Fangchang Wei, a local official position.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 開元二十三年 - A specific year during the reign of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 及第 - Passing the imperial examinations, achieving a degree.
  • 恃才傲物 - Relying on one's talent and being arrogant towards others.
  • 漫无与比 - Having no equal, being unparalleled.
  • 常自一壶逐胜郊野 - Often carrying a wine pot to seek pleasure in the countryside.
  • 偶憇于逆旅 - Temporarily resting at an inn.
  • 独酌独吟 - Drinking and reciting poetry alone.
  • 防有风雨暴至 - Suddenly encountering a storm.
  • 紫衣老人领一小童避雨于此 - An elderly man in purple clothing with a young boy seeking shelter.
  • 頴士见其散冗颇肆陵侮 - Seeing the man's disheveled appearance and behaving rudely towards him.
  • 逡巡风定雨霁 - Shortly after the wind stopped and the rain ceased.
  • 车马卒至 - Suddenly, a carriage and horses arrived.
  • 老人上马呼殿而去 - The elderly man mounted his horse, shouted, and left.
  • 頴士仓觇之 - Xing Shi looked around in surprise.
  • 左右曰吏部王尚书 - The attendants said it was the Minister of Personnel, Wang Shangshu.
  • 尚书名丘 - His name was Qiu.
  • 頴士常造门未之面 - Xing Shi had often visited but had never met him.
  • 极惊愕 - Extremely surprised.
  • 明日具长牋造门谢 - The next day, he prepared a long letter and went to apologize.
  • 丘命引至庑下坐责之 - Qiu ordered him to be led to a pavilion and scolded him.
  • 所恨与子非亲属当庭训之耳 - Although he regretted that they were not relatives, he should have disciplined him publicly.
  • 顷曰子负文学之名踞忽如此止于一第乎 - After a while, he said, 'You rely on your literary reputation and behave so arrogantly. Is passing the exam all you care about?'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 開元二十三年 - A significant year in the Tang Dynasty, known for its cultural and economic prosperity.
  • 及第 - Refers to passing the imperial examinations, which was a crucial step in the career of scholars and officials.
  • 恃才傲物 - A common theme in classical literature, highlighting the dangers of arrogance and overconfidence.
  • 漫无与比 - Emphasizes the exceptional nature of Xing Shi's talent.
  • 常自一壶逐胜郊野 - Reflects the lifestyle of literati who often sought inspiration and pleasure in nature.
  • 偶憇于逆旅 - Inn travel was a common practice for travelers and officials.
  • 独酌独吟 - Drinking and reciting poetry alone was a traditional pastime for scholars.
  • 防有风雨暴至 - Sudden storms were often used in classical literature to create dramatic moments.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 天寳中 - A time reference meaning 'during the Tianbao era,' referring to a period of the Tang dynasty (742–756 CE).
  • 诸公主相効进食 - 'The princesses imitated each other in eating,' or 'the princesses competed in elaborate feasts.'
  • 命中宫袁思艺为检校进食使 - 'The emperor ordered the palace to appoint Yuan Siyi as the inspector of food services.'
  • 水陆珍羞数千盘之费 - 'The cost of thousands of trays of delicacies from both land and sea.'
  • 盖中人十家之产 - 'This cost was equivalent to the combined wealth of ten households of palace attendants (中人).'
  • 中书舎人窦华甞因退朝遇公主进食方列于通衢 - 'Zhongshu Sheren Dou Hua once, after court, encountered the princesses holding a feast arranged on the main thoroughfare.'
  • 宫苑小儿数百人奋梃而前 - 'Hundreds of palace children rushed forward with sticks.'
  • 华仅以身免 - 'Dou Hua barely escaped with his life.'
  • 武惠妃生日 - 'On the birthday of Wu Huifei.'
  • 上与诸公主按舞于万歳楼下 - 'The emperor and the princesses performed a dance below the Wan'ai Pavilion.'
  • 上乘步辇从复道窥见卫士食毕以饼柑弃水窦中 - 'The emperor, riding in a palanquin, looked down from a covered walkway and saw the guards throwing leftover cakes and oranges into the water drain.'
  • 上大怒命高力士杖杀之 - 'The emperor was greatly angered and ordered Gao Lishi to beat them to death.'
  • 上方震怒左右无敢言者 - 'The emperor was in a rage, and no one dared to speak.'
  • 宁王从容请上曰 - 'Prince Ning calmly approached the emperor and said...'
  • 从复道窥见诸卫士之小过而杀之 - 'To punish the guards for a minor fault, having seen them from the covered walkway.'
  • 恐人臣不能自安又失大体 - 'This would make ministers unable to feel secure and would damage the dignity of the court.'
  • 陛下志在勤俭爱物恶弃于地奈何性命至重轻于残飱者乎 - 'Your Majesty's virtue is in frugality and love of objects; how can you treat human life so lightly compared to leftover food?'
  • 上蹶然悟遽命赦之 - 'The emperor suddenly realized his mistake and immediately ordered their release.'
  • 杜甫后漂寓湘潭间旅于衡州耒阳县颇为令长所厌 - 'Afterward, Du Fu drifted to Xiangtan, and while traveling in Hengzhou's Laiyang County, he was quite disliked by the local officials.'
  • 甫投诗于宰宰遂置牛炙白酒以遗甫饮过多一夕而卒 - 'Du Fu sent poems to the county magistrate, who then prepared roasted beef and white wine to give to Du Fu. Du Fu drank too much and died the next night.'
  • 集中犹有赠聂耒阳诗也 - 'In his collected poems, there is still a poem dedicated to Nie of Laiyang.'
  • 明皇杂録补遗 - 'Supplementary Notes from the Miscellaneous Records of Emperor Minghuang' — the title of the source text.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天寳中 - The *Tianbao* era was a time of prosperity and later decline during the Tang dynasty, particularly known for Emperor Xuanzong’s reign and the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 袁思艺 - Yuan Siyi was a palace official during the Tang dynasty, known for his role in managing imperial banquets and food services.
  • 中人 - Refers to palace attendants or eunuchs, a class of officials who served in the imperial court.
  • 中书舎人 - A high-ranking official in the *Zhongshu Province*, responsible for drafting imperial edicts and advising on state affairs.
  • 窦华 - Dou Hua, a *Zhongshu Sheren*, known for his role in the imperial court.
  • 武惠妃 - Wu Huifei was a favored concubine of Emperor Xuanzong, known for her influence and the jealousy she inspired among other concubines.
  • 万歳楼 - The *Wan'ai Pavilion*, a symbolic and architectural feature in the imperial palace complex.
  • 高力士 - Gao Lishi was a powerful eunuch during the Tang dynasty, known for his close relationship with Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 宁王 - Prince Ning, the younger brother of Emperor Xuanzong, known for his political acumen and patronage of the arts.
  • 杜甫 - Du Fu, one of the most revered poets of the Tang dynasty, known for his moral concerns and historical consciousness.
  • 湘潭 - A county in Hunan province, where Du Fu lived in exile.
  • 衡州耒阳县 - Laiyang County, located in Hengzhou, Hunan, where Du Fu spent time in his later years.
  • 聂耒阳 - Nie of Laiyang, likely a local official or friend of Du Fu to whom he dedicated a poem.
  • 明皇杂録补遗 - A historical text attributed to the Tang dynasty, recording anecdotes and miscellaneous notes about Emperor Xuanzong (also known as Minghuang).
